title: General Peer Bears Mentoring
content:
Peer BEARS Mentoring Program
Morgan State University - School of Computer, Mathematical, and Natural Sciences

The Peer B.E.A.R.S. (Bringing Enrichment and Rearing Success) Program showcases the talents and passions of upperclassmen in the SCMNS who mentor first-year students. The program aims to enhance student engagement and persistence through graduation by guiding freshmen as they navigate a new environment, rigorous coursework, and independence. This peer mentoring initiative focuses on improving study habits, science and math competency, and professional development through academic, professional, and social activities.

MENTORS
Peer BEARS mentors are upperclassmen (sophomores, juniors, seniors) who are certified College Reading and Learning Association (CRLA) Level 1 Peer Educators. They provide support to freshmen SCMNS majors throughout their first year.

Interested in joining the Peer BEARS mentor team?
Email: SCMNS-PeerBEARS@morgan.edu

MENTEES
Mentees are freshmen with majors in SCMNS, mentored throughout their first year. To apply, complete the mentee application form. After review, our Student Success Coordinator will contact you if a mentor is assigned.

How to Apply: Complete the mentee application form available through the SCMNS department.
